sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the green player is now right below the blue horizontal bar with gray interior. The white square that was at (36, 32) seems to have merged or moved. I can see the player is adjacent to the blue/gray bar now. Let me check the current state to understand what's happening and what the next step should be.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    b = (min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']), min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']))
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, pos={b}, children={n['children']}")

# Check green adjacency
green = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
adj = current_frame.segmentation['adjacency_list']
green_id = green['id']
print(f"\nGreen adjacents:")
for pair in adj:
    if green_id in pair:
        other = pair[0] if pair[1] == green_id else pair[1]
        other_node = seg[other]
        print(f"  Node {other}: color={other_node['color']}, pixels={other_node['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
